from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, single sentence.
    The function groups information by subject and renders each predicate
    with a natural‑language phrase. When several predicates share the same
    subject they are combined into a relative clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase. The lambda receives the object
    # and returns a string that can be inserted after the subject.
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": lambda o: f'also known as {o}',
        "bodyStyle": lambda o: f'has a {o} body style',
        "engine": lambda o: f'is powered by {o}',
        "manufacturer": lambda o: f'was manufactured by {o}',
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": lambda o: f'is related to {o}',
        "transmission": lambda o: f'features a {o} transmission',
        "wheelbase": lambda o: f'has a wheelbase of {o}',
        "builder": lambda o: f'was built by {o}',
        "completionDate": lambda o: f'was completed on {o}',
        "length": lambda o: f'is {o} long',
        "powerType": lambda o: f'uses {o} as power type',
        "shipClass": lambda o: f'belongs to the {o} class',
        "shipDisplacement": lambda o: f'displaces {o} tonnes',
        "shipLaunch": lambda o: f'was launched on {o}',
        "shipOrdered": lambda o: f'was ordered on {o}',
        "shipPower": lambda o: f'has power {o}',
        "topSpeed": lambda o: f'can reach a top speed of {o} knots',
        "location": lambda o: f'is located in {o}',
        "christeningDate": lambda o: f'was christened on {o}',
        "maidenVoyage": lambda o: f'undertook its maiden voyage on {o}',
        "owner": lambda o: f'is owned by {o}',
        "shipBeam": lambda o: f'has a beam of {o} metres',
        "shipInService": lambda o: f'entered service on {o}",
        "status": lambda o: f'has status {o}',
        "activeYearsStartDate": lambda o: f'has been active since {o}",
        "shipLaidDown": lambda o: f'was laid down on {o}",
        "buildDate": lambda o: f'was built between {o}",
        "cylinderCount": lambda o: f'has {o} cylinders',
        "totalProduction": lambda o: f'produced {o} units',
        "countryOrigin": lambda o: f'originated from {o}",
        "diameter": lambda o: f'has a diameter of {o}",
        "failedLaunches": lambda o: f'experienced {o} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": lambda o: f'has {o} stages',
        "totalLaunches": lambda o: f'completed {o} launch(es)",
        "assembly": lambda o: f'was assembled in {o}",
        "class": lambda o: f'belongs to the {o} class",
        "designer": lambda o: f'was designed by {o}",
        "modelYears": lambda o: f'was produced in {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f'is a country called {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda o: f'was founded in {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f'was founded by {o}",
        "designCompany": lambda o: f'was designed by {o}",
        "productionStartYear": lambda o: f'production started in {o}",
        "width": lambda o: f'is {o} metres wide",
        "layout": lambda o: f'has a layout of {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda o: f'is a subsidiary of {o}",
        "operator": lambda o: f'is operated by {o}",
        "product": lambda o: f'produces {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f'is based in {o}",
        "successor": lambda o: f'was succeeded by {o}",
        "fate": lambda o: f'ended up as {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda o: f'has key person {o}",
        "subsidiary": lambda o: f'owns subsidiary {o}",
        "comparable": lambda o: f'is comparable to {o}",
        "finalFlight": lambda o: f'last flew on {o}",
        "function": lambda o: f'functions as {o}",
        "launchSite": lambda o: f'launches from {o}",
        "maidenFlight": lambda o: f'first flew on {o}",
        "capital": lambda o: f'has capital {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f'people are called {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f'is led by {o}",
        "partialFailures": lambda o: f'had {o} partial failures",
        "site": lambda o: f'is located at {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f'has headquarters in {o}",
        "associatedRocket": lambda o: f'is associated with {o}",
        "saint": lambda o: f'patron saint is {o}",
        "employer": lambda o: f'works for {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda o: f'has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f'speaks {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f'leader holds the title {o}",
        "anthem": lambda o: f'anthem is {o}",
        "productionEndYear": lambda o: f'production ended in {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f'has division {o}",
        "shipDraft": lambda o: f'has a draft of {o}",
        "modelStartYear": lambda o: f'production started in {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda o: f'covers an area of {o} km²",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f'is part of {o}",
        "extinctionDate": lambda o: f'ceased to exist on {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        phrase_func = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, None)
        if phrase_func:
            phrase = phrase_func(t.object)
        else:
            # fallback: turn predicate into readable words
            pred_readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
            phrase = f'{pred_readable} {t.object}'
        subject_map[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build sentences for each subject
    sentences = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_map.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            sentences.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}.")
        else:
            # first phrase as main clause, rest as relative clauses
            main = phrases[0]
            relatives = ", ".join(f"which {p}" for p in phrases[1:-1])
            if relatives:
                clause = f"{subj} {main}, {relatives}, and {phrases[-1]}."
            else:
                clause = f"{subj} {main} and {phrases[-1]}."
            sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into one paragraph‑style sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0]
    else:
        return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
      Each triple is rendered as “subject predicate object”.
      Multiple triples are combined with commas and an ‘and’ before the last one.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # Build simple clauses for each triple
      clauses = []
      for t in triples:
          # Ensure objects that look like numbers are not quoted unnecessarily
          obj = t.object
          # Simple clause: subject predicate object
          clauses.append(f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}")
      # Combine clauses into a readable sentence
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          sentence = clauses[0] + "."
      else:
          sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
      return sentence
